concerns.text: The 'Verified Datasets' block lists only text-based LLM training corpora (OLScience Guanaco Format) which are explicitly marked 'NOT SUITABLE' for spatial analysis. The plan relies on a 'Synthetic Data Generation' strategy to satisfy the spec's requirement for real-world noise, traffic, and land-use data (FR-001, US-1). This creates a fundamental mismatch: the research question asks for analysis of 'Publicly Available Urban Noise Pollution Data,' but the only available verified resources are synthetic proxies. The plan cannot validate the pipeline against real-world noise patterns or covariates as required by the spec.

The 'Critical Gap Analysis' admits a 'BLOCKING GAP' where no verified geospatial noise dataset exists in the provided list. The proposed solution (generating synthetic data) changes the nature of the project from an analysis of public data to a simulation study. This violates the core intent of the spec (FR-001: 'ingest noise data from citizen science archives...') and the Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which requires fetching external datasets from canonical sources. A synthetic generator cannot fulfill the requirement to analyze 'publicly available' data.
